name: deployment-patterns
description: Deployment patterns: Vercel, Docker, environment configuration, monitoring, logging, and production readiness

entries.title: Vercel Deployment Configuration
entries.content: Vercel auto-detects Next.js. Configure via vercel.json or project settings. Environment variables in dashboard. Serverless functions in app/api/. Edge functions with runtime: edge. Preview deployments for PRs.

entries.title: Docker Multi-Stage Build for Next.js
entries.content: Multi-stage build: deps stage (install), build stage (compile), runner stage (minimal production image). Use standalone output for smaller images. Set NODE_ENV=production.
entries.code:
# Dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci --production=false

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1

RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs
RUN adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs

COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static

USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
ENV PORT=3000
CMD ["node", "server.js"]

# next.config.js — enable standalone output
// output: 'standalone'

# Build & run
# docker build -t myapp .
# docker run -p 3000:3000 --env-file .env.production myapp

entries.title: Health Check & Monitoring Endpoints
entries.content: Create /api/health endpoint for load balancers and monitoring. Check DB connectivity, external services. Return status, version, uptime. Use for Kubernetes liveness/readiness probes.

entries.title: Structured Logging for Production
entries.content: Use structured JSON logging in production. Include: timestamp, level, message, requestId, userId. Use correlation IDs across services. Log to stdout (container-friendly). Avoid console.log in production.
